data.title: Foot Locker sees 2.6% drop in comparable sales in Q1
data.description:
Foot Locker (NYSE:FL) said its first-quarter comparable sales dropped 2.6% as compared to the same period last year, as the firm saw softer traffic trends globally.

The firm said it expects its adjusted net loss per share to be $0.07 in the quarter, as compared to adjusted earnings per share of $0.22 in Q1 2024.

Wall Street estimates the firm's net loss per share for Q1 to be $0.02.

The company expects net loss to be $363 million, as compared with net income of $8 million in the corresponding prior-year period.

Separately, the firm said DICK'S will acquire Foot Locker in a deal that implies an equity value of ~$2.4 billion and an enterprise value of ~$2.5 billion.

Shares of the firm soared 81% in premarket trade, driven mainly by the acquisition agreement.

data.title: Bon Natural Life to conduct reverse stock split
data.description:
• Bon Natural Life (NASDAQ:BON) said it will implement a 1-for-25 reverse stock split of its authorized, issued, and outstanding class A ordinary shares.
  • The split is intended to bring the firm into compliance with the minimum bid price requirement in order to maintain its listing on Nasdaq.

data.title: AtkinsRéalis Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.57, revenue of C$2.55B; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• AtkinsRéalis press release (OTCPK:SNCAF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.57.
  • Revenue of C$2.55B (+12.8% Y/Y).
  • The company's total backlog also continued to be robust with a 17% increase compared to December 31, 2024 and a 31% increase compared to March 31, 2024.
  • Net cash generated from operating activities of C$39.3 million.
  • Net limited recourse and recourse debt to Adjusted EBITDA ratio was 1.1 as at March 31, 2025, in line with December 31, 2024 and lower than the ratio of 1.7 as at March 31, 2024.

  • 2025 Outlook (Revised)

    The Company is raising its Nuclear revenue outlook for full year 2025 to between C$1.9 billion and C$2.0 billion, from the previous range of between C$1.6 billion and C$1.7 billion, reflecting strong growth year-to-date and confidence in demand continuing over the balance of the year, supported by its record backlog. The Company is also adjusting its Nuclear Segment Adjusted EBIT to segment revenue ratio outlook for full year 2025 to between 11% and 13%, from the previous range of between 12% and 14%, reflective of the 2025 expected business mix.

    All other financial outlook metrics for full year 2025, issued on March 13, 2025 in the Q4 2024 press release, are maintained.

    2025 – 2027 Financial Targets (Revised)

    At its Investor Day held on June 13, 2024, the Company unveiled its 2025 – 2027 Deliver Excellence, Drive Growth Strategic Plan, and at the same time provided certain financial targets for 2025 through 2027. As a result of the strong financial and operating performance of the Nuclear segment during the first quarter of 2025, the significant increase in the Nuclear backlog and the Company's optimistic outlook regarding the global demand for the services offered by the Nuclear segment in the mid and long terms, the Company is raising its Nuclear annual revenue target to between C$2.2 billion and C$2.5 billion by 2027, from the previous range of between C$1.8 billion and C$2.0 billion.

    All other financial targets for 2025 – 2027, as announced on June 13, 2024, are maintained.

data.title: Klaviyo prices 11M share secondary offering
data.description:
• Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) priced an underwritten public offering of ~11M shares of its Series A common stock by Andrew Bialecki, the company’s CEO and co-founder, at a public offering price of $34.00 per share.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the shares being offered by Mr. Bialecki.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 16, 2025.
  • The stock price dropped 1.15% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Commodity roundup: crude oil sinks on demand concerns, Gold haven bets fall
data.description:
With signs of waning demand and oversupply, crude oil price dropped 3.93% to $60.69 per barrel on Thursday. Brent crude oil futures sank over 3% to below $64 per barrel on as global oversupply concerns weighed. Reports indicated that Iran is open to a deal with the U.S. in exchange for sanctions relief, raising expectations that an agreement could increase Iranian oil inflows back into the global market. Saudi Arabia also voiced full support for the US-Iran nuclear talks.

Furthermore, the International Energy Agency (IEA) projects global oil demand growth to slow from 990,000 barrels per day ((bpd)) in the first quarter of 2025 to 650,000 bpd for the remainder of the year, as economic headwinds and record EV sales curb use. Crude inventory increased by 3.5M barrels.

Natural gas was slightly lwer as the focus shifted from U.S.-China trade tensions to weak underlying demand in Asia. The US dollar index (DXY) traded 0.22% lower to 100.82.

In precious metals, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) dropped 0.52%, trading around $3,170 per ounce as haven demand for the yellow precious metal fades.

"China’s affinity for gold continues to strengthen, with a shift from physical to investment demand amid the challenging geopolitical backdrop. Trading volumes on Chinese exchanges surge, while inflows in gold-backed ETFs remain strong. This has pushed the onshore premium to USD50/oz in recent weeks." wrote senior commodity strategist Daniel Hynes.

ETFs related to commodities: (NYSEARCA:USO), (NYSEARCA:BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (NYSEARCA:USL), (NYSEARCA:DBO), (NYSEARCA:DRIP), (NYSEARCA:GUSH), (NASDAQ:USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (NYSEARCA:BOIL), (NYSEARCA:KOLD), (NYSEARCA:UNL), (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (NYSEARCA:GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (NYSEARCA:PHYS), (NYSEARCA:GLDM), (BATS:AAAU), (NYSEARCA:SGOL), (NASDAQ:RING), (NYSEARCA:BAR), (NYSEARCA:OUNZ), (NYSEARCA:SLV), (NYSEARCA:PSLV), (NYSEARCA:SIVR), (NYSEARCA:SIL), (NYSEARCA:SILJ)

data.title: Xunlei Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.004, revenue of $88.76M
data.description:
• Xunlei press release (NASDAQ:XNET): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.004.
  • Revenue of $88.76M (+10.4% Y/Y).
  • Subscription revenues were US$35.7 million, representing an increase of 7.7% year-over-year.
  • Live-streaming and other services revenues were US$28.4 million, representing an increase of 66.0% year-over-year.
  • Cloud computing revenues were US$24.7 million, representing a decrease of 18.0% year-over-year.
  • For the second quarter of 2025, Xunlei estimates total revenues to be between US$91 million and US$96 million, and the midpoint of the range represents a quarter-over-quarter increase of approximately 5.3%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Alibaba falls short in FQ4; Free cash flow drops 76% Y/Y
Stock index futures fall ahead of slew of economic releases including PPI data
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday as investors turned their focus to a slew of macroeconomic releases slated to land later in the day, including the wholesale inflation report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.04%.

Wall Street on Wednesday eked out a gain, extending a two-day rally and moving further into positive territory for the year.

"The strong recent equity rally showed some signs of exhaustion over the past 24 hours, even though gains for the tech mega caps helped the S&P 500 extend its gains since the closing low on April 7 to +18.26%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Donald Trump’s Middle East tour continued to generate headlines yesterday, but their market impact was narrower in comparison to Tuesday’s rally, Sidorov added.

The main event for the day will be the April PPI release before the opening bell. Core PPI is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and 3.1% Y/Y.

PPI is expected to come in at 2.5% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.

The initial jobless claims and the April retail sales will also land at the same time. The former is expected to rise to 229K.

Core retail sales is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and retail sales at 0.0%.

The May Philly Fed Business Outlook and the May Empire State Mfg Survey will also land before the opening bell. 

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will give a speech at the Thomas Laubach Research Conference concerning Framework Review.

data.title: Undocumented communication devices found in Chinese solar inverters - report
data.description:
Rogue communication devices have been found in some Chinese solar power inverters and batteries, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters, raising concerns of potential security risks in U.S. renewable energy infrastructure.

Power inverters are used to connect solar panels and wind turbines to electricity grids. While such inverters allow remote access for updates and maintenance, utility companies usually install firewalls to block direct communication back to China.

However, undocumented communication devices have reportedly been found in some Chinese solar power inverters. And over the past nine months, such devices—including cellular radios—have also been found in some batteries from Chinese suppliers.

These devices could allow firewalls to be evaded remotely to switch off inverters or change their settings, which could destabilize power grids. "That effectively means there is a built-in way to physically destroy the grid," one of the sources told Reuters.

A Department of Energy spokesperson said there were challenges with manufacturers disclosing and documenting functionalities. "While this functionality may not have malicious intent, it is critical for those procuring to have a full understanding of the capabilities of the products received," they said, adding that work is ongoing to address disclosure gaps.

"We oppose the generalization of the concept of national security, distorting and smearing China's infrastructure achievements," a spokesperson for the Chinese embassy in the U.S. said.

U.S. utilities are preparing to ban procurement of inverters from Chinese firms, sources told Reuters, or minimize the use of Chinese inverters—like Florida Power & Light Company.

data.title: HIVE Digital exceeds 8 EH/s of bitcoin hashrate
data.description:
HIVE Digital (NASDAQ:HIVE) has surpassed 8.0 exahash per second (EH/s) of global bitcoin mining hashrate, marking a milestone in the company's strategic expansion toward its 25 EH/s target for calendar Q4 2025.

As of May 15, the company’s total operating hashrate stood at 8.3 EH/s. HIVE Digital (NASDAQ:HIVE) said it remains on track to reach its Phase 1 objective of 11.5 EH/s by the end of June 2025, with additional capacity expected to come online in the weeks ahead.

This rapid growth is being driven by the energization of HIVE's first 100MW hydro-powered facility in Yguazu, Paraguay. Its second 100MW in Yguazu, scheduled for completion this summer, is expected to provide the infrastructure for growth from 11.5 EH/s to 18 EH/s.

Separately, the firm entered into an amended and restated equity distribution agreement, pursuant to which it may sell up to $119.23M of common shares.

Under its previous equity distribution agreement, HIVE sold common shares for aggregate proceeds of around $180.77M.

Source: Press Release
